meaning: n.仪式,典礼,习俗,惯例,礼拜式,教派
examples: The programme concluded with Stravinsky's 'Rite of Spring'.
It used to be that a paper route or an after-school job at the local grocer was viewed as a rite of passage for young people.
It was considered a rite of passage to own a broken down vehicle.
Today, The Rite of Spring remains a classic, but it also still packs a punch.
On Jan. 11, the filing date, Rite Aid announced it would suspend financial reporting until July.
